title: Sia-Lan Wezz

{{Quote|It is ''you'' who will die!|Sia-Lan Wezz to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] seconds before her death|<ref name="Purge" />}}
'''Sia-Lan Wezz''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] in the waning years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the former [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Lo-Jad]].

==Biography==
[[File:WookieeToss.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Rorworr, Vor'en Kurn, and Sia-Lan Wezz deal with a [[T4 turret droid]] ambush in close quarters.]]
Discovered to be [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] during infancy, Sia-Lan Wezz was taken to the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] for [[Jedi training/Legends|formal training]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] at the [[Jedi academy|Temple's academy]]. After years of training, Wezz was selected by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Lo-Jad]] as a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] and was destined to travel [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] with him to continue her studies down the Jedi Path. A year after her thirteenth birthday, Wezz was assigned to investigate the Coruscant-based terrorist organization known as [[the Flail]]. Teaming with a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]], Wezz was forced to engage members of the group and went on to complete her mission.<ref name="SOC">{{WizCite|story=Shadows of Coruscant|book=Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook}}</ref> In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Master Lo-Jad assigned Wezz to [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] where she was to study for a semester at the [[Royal House of Learning/Legends|Royal House of Learning]] in [[Theed/Legends|Theed]]. Becoming close with [[Headmaster/Legends|headmaster]] [[Lucos Dannt]], Wezz was on planet when the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[Blockade/Legends|blockaded]] the planet. During the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]], Wezz joined the [[Naboo Underground]], a resistance movement that attempted to topple Federation control. During her time in the resistance, Wezz became well acquainted with the likes of [[Deel Surool]], [[Rorworr (Naboo)|Rorworr]], and fellow Padawan [[Rann I-Kanu]]. While Wezz would leave Naboo shortly after the Federation was defeated, she kept in touch with her new comrades and would involve them in several missions in her future career.<ref name="Invasion of Theed" />

During her time as a Padawan, Wezz either crafted several [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] or possessed several [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystals]] as she was seen with blue, pink, yellow, and green-bladed weapons throughout her service to the Order. Following the election of [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] as [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]], Wezz was assigned a mission to the snow-covered world of [[Puloorn]] with her comrades Rorworr, [[Vor'en Kurn]], and Deel Surool to uncover a hidden [[Sith temple (Puloorn)|Sith Temple]]. The team found the temple ruins, possibly inhabited by [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] beasts or cultists, and reported the findings to Palpatine and the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]]. Over the course of the next decade, Wezz would study and prepare for her [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Trials of Knighthood]], a series of tests she would pass before being elevated to the rank of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]].<ref name="SWRG">''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook]]''</ref>

Just before the start of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Wezz and her comrades journeyed to [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] where the four foiled a Separatist assassination attempt on a [[Corulag/Legends|Corulag]] [[Senator/Legends|Senator]], [[Alastar Treen]].<ref name="RPGRCRB">''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Revised Core Rulebook]]''</ref> Following this, the four frequently worked together. The team faced a variety of opponents and situations, such as attacks on battle droid commanders, the investigation of a bounty hunter operating out of the [[Royal Hutt Hotel]], assaults on crime bosses, encounters with [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], cantina brawls with [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]]<ref name="RPGCRB"> ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game Core Rulebook]]';</ref> and Rodian gangs, an escape from a battle droid [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]],<ref name="RPGRCRB" /> an escape from a [[Colicoid/Legends|Colicoid]]-run prison in the [[Spice mines of Kessel/Legends|Spice Mines of Kessel]],<ref name="GOI">''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]''</ref> a [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] ambush in an abandoned armory, and even [[E-Web heavy repeating blaster/Legends|mounted blasters]] in the darkened corridors of the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Grumbog]]'s [[Space station/Legends|space station]] [[Grumbog's space station fortress|fortress]].<ref name="RPGRCRB" />

[[File:Sia-Lan-Wezzdeath.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Sia-Lan Wezz [[Shiak|impaled]] by Darth Vader]]
The team was so successful that they continued to receive orders directly from the office of the Chancellor and the High Council. One such mission had the group investigating the disappearance of a Republic arsenal ship that had been tasked with decommissioning the Trade Federation Army defeated at Naboo. The investigation led the team to [[Kwenn Space Station/Legends|Kwenn Space Station]], where they defeated several re-activated battle droids.<ref name="RPGCRB" /> Another mission to which the team was assigned involved preventing [[Corann]] from being the first of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] to withdraw from the Republic. Rorworr's team investigated rumors of Separatist activity on the small industrial planet and discovered a droid manufacturing plant.<ref name="RPGRCRB" />

An accomplished Guardian by the end of the war, Wezz was able to avoid the execution of [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|Contingency]] [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] at the end of the war; an order issued by the [[Treason/Legends|treasonous]] Chancellor Palpatine ordering the death of all Jedi. Fleeing from the Grand Army, Wezz came into contact with fellow survivor Master [[Shadday Potkin/Legends|Shadday Potkin]] and agreed to meet her on [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. A month after the birth of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Wezz [[Conclave on Kessel|met]] with Jedi Potkin, [[Ma'kis'shaalas/Legends|Ma'kis'shaalas]], [[Roblio Darté]], [[Tsui Choi/Legends|Tsui Choi]], [[Bultar Swan/Legends|Bultar Swan]], [[Koffi Arana]], and [[Jastus Farr]] to discuss their next move against Palpatine, who was indeed a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. Unbeknownst to all present, Potkin had arrogantly lured [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to the meeting in hopes that the team could overwhelm the Sith and end the Empire. Sia-Lan Wezz was the first to attack Vader, charging toward him with her lightsaber poised high. She was no match for the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] though, who easily extended his lightsaber through her chest, and sliced off her hand before her [[Death/Legends|dead]] body hit the floor.<ref name="Purge" />

===Legacy===
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Sia-Lan Wezz and fellow Jedi [[Dorn Tavers]] were portrayed on a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] poster under the slogan "Remember [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]."<ref name="Hero's Guide">''[[Hero's Guide]]''</ref> Reports erroneously indicated that Wezz was killed by [[High Inquisitor]] [[Volytar]] during the [[Battle of Skorrupon]], defending [[Skorrupon/Legends|Skorrupon's]] people from being taken over by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces.<ref name="Galactic Campaign Guide">''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Sian-lan_wezz.jpg|thumb|left|145px|Sia-Lan Wezz]]
Sia-Lan Wezz is the iconic [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|Jedi Guardian]] for the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'', the character used for every game example within the rulebooks. For some reason, an article in ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 91]]'' referred to her character as "Dane."

Sia-Lan Wezz's death was originally established in ''[[Galactic Campaign Guide]]'', during the [[Battle of Skorrupon]]. Later, however, [[Purge (comic book)|''Star Wars: Purge'']] established that she was killed by Darth Vader shortly after the formation of the Galactic Empire. ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' confirmed her death at the hands of Vader.

In [[Purge (comic book)|''Star Wars: Purge'']], Sia-Lan's lightsaber blade changes color from green to pink when Darth Vader slices her hand off. Other sources have depicted her with a yellow or blue lightsaber.
